title: anthoxanthum horsfieldii var. sumatranum
intro: Anthoxanthum horsfieldii var. sumatranum, also known as Sumatran oats, is a variety of the Anthoxanthum horsfieldii plant, which is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Sumatran oats include its ability to reduce fever, improve digestion, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin and wound infections.
